steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: “[Blank] though it seemed to many mathematicians, the Marden tameness conjecture … eventually yielded.” The phrase though it seemed sets up a contrast: whatever the blank describes is what the conjecture appeared to be, but the outcome contradicts it. Because the conjecture was eventually solved, the blank must describe something that makes solving it seem impossible or surprising.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Insuperable': means impossible to overcome. “Insuperable though it seemed” contrasts directly with “eventually yielded”—the conjecture looked unsolvable but was solved. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Unequivocal': means absolutely clear or certain. A conjecture that is clear and then gets proved is not a meaningful contrast—proof is the expected outcome. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Irreproachable': means beyond criticism or faultless. A faultless conjecture being proved creates no logical tension. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Ineluctable': means inevitable. If the conjecture seemed inevitable, its eventual solution would be expected, not surprising. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
